one_line: Queen of Ullal who defied Portuguese tribute demands and defended the Karnataka coast.

appearance.age_look: Late 20s to early 30s, athletic and wiry with a compact, battle-hardened build.
appearance.face: Oval face with strong cheekbones, warm medium-brown coastal Karnataka complexion, straight nose, slightly arched brows, sharp dark eyes with a determined gaze; default expression is calm, alert resolve with a hint of defiance at the mouth.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair oiled and drawn back into a tight, low bun at the nape covered by a deep-red cotton headscarf (mundu-style wrap) tied firmly around the head, with a few short strands at the temples loose from sweat and battle.
appearance.costume: A practical warrior-queen version of a 16th‑century coastal Karnataka sari: unpatterned deep indigo cotton sari worn in a tightly wrapped kachche (between-the-legs) drape ending mid-calf, border trimmed with a narrow burnt-orange stripe; matching plain indigo short-sleeved ravike (blouse) fitted close to the torso; a simple dark-brown leather belt around the waist holding a small cloth pouch; bare feet with sturdy calloused soles, ankles adorned with thin brass anklets; minimal jewellery—small gold nose stud on the left, tiny round gold earrings, and a plain narrow brass bangle on each wrist; a short, sleeveless, quilted cotton war-vest in muted coconut-brown over the blouse, secured with two cloth ties at the front, lightly padded to suggest concealed armour while remaining flexible for movement.
appearance.signature: A medium-length steel tulwar with a slightly curving single-edged blade, simple brass hilt with disc pommel, carried unsheathed in the right hand and an undecorated dark-brown leather scabbard slung diagonally across her back by a plain indigo cloth strap.

ref_desc: Rani Abbakka Chowta is a late-20s athletic woman with a medium-brown oval face, sharp dark eyes, black hair in a low bun under a deep-red headscarf, wearing a deep indigo kachche-draped sari with burnt-orange border, coconut-brown sleeveless war-vest, minimal brass-gold jewellery, bare feet with anklets, and carrying a simple brass-hilted tulwar with leather back-scabbard in a deep indigo, burnt orange, coconut brown, brass gold, and dark brown leather palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Rani Abbakka Chowta: a late-20s athletic coastal Karnataka queen with an oval medium-brown face, strong cheekbones, straight nose, sharp dark eyes and a calm, defiant expression, her thick black hair pulled into a tight low bun covered by a deep-red cotton headscarf tied firmly around her head with a few loose temple strands; she wears a historically accurate 16th-century coastal Karnataka outfit—a plain deep indigo cotton sari in a practical kachche warrior drape ending mid-calf, with a narrow burnt-orange border, over a fitted short-sleeved indigo blouse, and a sleeveless quilted coconut-brown cotton war-vest tied at the front over her torso; a simple dark-brown leather belt sits at her waist holding a small cloth pouch, her feet are bare with sturdy soles and thin brass anklets, her jewellery limited to a tiny gold nose stud on the left, small round gold earrings and narrow brass bangles on each wrist; in her right hand she holds a medium-length slightly curved steel tulwar with a simple brass hilt and disc pommel, while its plain dark-brown leather scabbard hangs diagonally across her back on an indigo cloth strap; use a palette dominated by deep indigo, burnt orange, coconut brown, brass gold and dark brown leather, and pose her in neutral, front, side and back views emphasizing consistent face, hair, costume details and weapon design.
